| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: A seemingly minor misstep by President Trump has set off a chain reaction that’s shaking Washington to its core. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | The political world is in shock. | A seemingly minor has set off a chain reaction that’s shaking Washington to its core. Behind closed doors, lawmakers are whispering about impeachment ... while powerful insiders scramble to contain the fallout. What started as a small oversight could now — and the future of America as we know it. Some say this could be the turning point of a generation. Others say it’s already too late. — before it disappears from the headlines. This ad is sent on behalf of American Alternative Assets at 21550 W Oxnard St Ste 845, Woodland Hills, California 91367. body:
Venezuelans living in Texas [celebrated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdKD14phQuPgbuvztlcjYS7DO4MheQnJPf-KKe4ErRyN9mm8-buPNhhi2-3-7kuHXnMaNjhKJmfL4pI2-doq2cA6flMk6Dmkr-4iAugZZw2X0NsT3qCpvJvC9rMjITndaKqVVtRjZKpygbxE6cuimSWmQLcnrLC9X7fsqmBMGEi4SQQI2NGvvNXrsZguTskIB-cApN7wQ08uWOb7Tk3Lj0lpFszA_imj_CRwEDsdxNNvk_HyctcDh9BE4xORsPGPKvNQnZqt__bZSO4o0ZctUr9bvOZXa2QfvmQQOba9brXqBQ5WNfxwhxlB0JxTcVxvizw7-23tPg98BAAD__-CjhSw) the capture of their home country's dictator and his wife by U.S. special forces on Saturday. [Socialist demonstrators](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOozAURb8GOiPb6xhSuNiGLttuiYz9HJ7G8JD9CEm-fpT5hamvdHXO-X-bbh4zbvd_fgXHz0TTBmfNwAxlesAG7wOyr1zwCwr4wEhbPSYttRVSCXmZosf8mkwb3SzjVbXgVG97q4yxpl2cHwB0usxBJXuFXg2994MJaUgRbLDXFt3nTCp5UVoqqTtINkaro-6Tl7PxjZEQkal0vADD09eUX_SA0gVa2-wW5r02f_42emz0eJ5nl-j5sfjsjR53ysgYaqPHCoG2KFKhjcVCp6gU0GesLALkLI4qVpox4xui2AuJ1cejkEhELCrliFCqOJEX3ITSYqGj1La4uJ-YM1bbN0beV4_5h41_V_Th9HcAAAD__7EEkIM) in Dallas and Austin protested the military intervention. Texas Republicans widely [support](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb8Gd1gwJjgpKF7jLq_d0sIwjkdLjAWTONmvXyVa7Q9sfaSje-7HeTx7SrRe_vsrOn7MeVxxrwmZsYx3XPHrhslXLvSJBX1gymu9jaDASqWlOozRU3qOpoluUvGkG3S6t73VxljTLO4UZjx6fbJHM6kjaDOprpusVX3QpoOuIfeSKa0OGpRW0OJsY7QQoZ-9mowXRmEkzqXlBRkfvs7pme9Y2pCvTXIL81ZF90_AIGDY970NS8nriwoYtpyIKVQBgy9MIaGA4W2RP4j8WuW7TXKWv80yb1j8q1eCht6YXrfbsjXFxW2nlKjaXhh1uXpK7yX8t__uDr4DAAD__yFshnw) the move, while Democrats generally [oppose](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLEOmzAQQL_GbEb2xTFi8NDFW7p2RBd8hFMMRrYJSb--IpX6A11uOendvffrNtyQI6-Pn7iQq-8pDSsdJVKtlIcXrfR7p4ilZn5SJhwrp7XsAyiwUmmprkNAjp_BNMHdVeh1Q053trPaGGua2QWYOkCkcDHWaFR91_dkNI1aBRyVbtidMKXVVYPSClqabAgWAnQTqrtBYRQFrim3daZKbyxT_KQX5XZMSxPdXOtWxOWHAC_AH8fRPgvWcynAnzYCfEwjRgH-PCXAK32OiwD_5cktRa48Mq5Ffi1lTXIv8q93kbzKfy3kiFvdM8k0yQXDnpPENciZizx4IgG-yS5sB8fIxXbCqMeCHL_P1v9L_HLwJwAA___H_ZIK) it.

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1854, the Texas and Red River Telegraph Company was chartered as the [first telegraph company](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFy6yAQgOHToM4aWCN4Liheo85pU2qQWKQdY_DAWrJz-kxyhVzg_2e-z-t09ZQorx_-jo5fsUwZj5aQGeu0Y8avJybfuNINK_qFqeT2nECCOUl1ksMUPKX3pLvgZhkuqkOnrLFGaW10tzm7mIigZgsSox_OMVz-gb4MZx3jbKXqyP3EpJKDAqkk9BhNCAYC2OjlrL3QEgNxqT1vyPjyLaZ32bH2S7l3yW3MjybO_wWMAsbjOHpumy85Uca-1FXAuPkc5lJuAkbMXAmbgJEx4Vr9Yzs1rDst2FUXHgelRM1YoeV695R-J_w3mt3BdwAAAP__pct6yA) in Texas. Its first office was opened in Marshall the following month. A father and son in Deep East Texas have [teamed up](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOGyEURb-G6Z4FLMvEBUWa6TZtytEbeBhiBix4Hq_z9ZFXyhdseXWlo3vP74_1A3PJ9fILd3L8Gdta6TEKMVNfD6r0904FB_d8pU7oObc67quW2oJUIN_XgLk8VzMFt8lwVhM5NdvZKmOsmZKTROf5bKRWEhW9eRXRxm0LJvyIwUc5ZfeCSSXflZZK6hNFG4LVQc8R5WZQGEkhc-snTsT0iSOWZzuon3zbp-IS822It59CL0Iv1xj2VyH08noi9FKaxyL0EpETdcAaYLQKO14JvnCQ8uDWn4ADRqKeYxzQIlTKl7S1nusFfLtXzjTAt8KtQsIDewCf7v76P_zBcaMOlR7c6tRduD1yKXnYWRh52TGXr8X8Pc-H0_8CAAD__772mDg) in a unique way to keep their communities safe. Get the details in today's _Et Cetera_ section. ## Texans React to Maduro's Removal
Venezuelans living in Texas [celebrated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdKD14phQuPgbuvztlcjYS7DO4MheQnJPf-KKe4ErRyN9mm8-buPNhhi2-3-7kuHXnMaNjhKJmfL4pI2-doq2cA6flMk6Dmkr-4iAugZZw2X0NsT3qCpvJvC9rMjITndaKqVVtRjZKpygbxE6cuimSWmQLcnrLC9X7fsqmBMGEi4SQQI2NGvvNXrsZguTskIB-cApN7wQ08uWOb7Tk3Lj0lpFszA_imj_CRwEDsdxNNvk_HyctcDh9BE4xORsPGPKvNQnZqt__bZSO4o0ZctUr9bvOZXa2QfvmQQOba9brXqBQ5WNfxwhxlB0JxTcVxvizw7-23tPg98BAAD__-CjhSw) the capture of their home country's dictator and his wife by U.S. special forces on Saturday. ## Houston METRO Bus Flips Over
A Houston METRO bus [flipped over](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAURL8Gd1hwIdgpKLZxl223tHC42GixieA6JPv1qzy9P3jtaDSjc_7c5puLKR7rb7ejpVfI84GtJiTCMj_xwH8nJlepxL9Y0N0p5qOeMwgwXEguLrN3Mb1n3Xm7CH-VHVo5mMFIrY3uNuuuizbaKDVoOYwm-Is04JT3OCqtFHbRfsaEFBcJQgroMRjvDXgYghOLdkwL9JFy6WlDwperIb3zE0t_z3uX7Eb0qEz9YjAxmFprfcgvMFs-K-XjU2IwfagYTN8h35FK5stZeXApVb7FdWvuzWEc-X5fc8ODVyqI1BXrHy2mFKsZmBbr7mL6eqaf-Xpa-B8AAP__Ux6Brg) and landed on a highway overpass embankment after it was reportedly rear-ended on Saturday. Fourteen people were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, but all of them are now "safe and out of danger," according to an agency spokesperson. The bus was stuck on an embankment next to McGowen Street and State Highway 288 for several hours after the crash. While the driver and most passengers were able to exit the bus on their own, firefighters had to use the Jaws of Life and "almost every cutting tool that we had" to [remove one rider](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGu4yAUheHV4I4IMAa7oJjGXaad0sJwie8MBguwk8zqn_K28OojfTr6_9yXu8WI6fHb7mDaK-QlwbNGaA3KckGC_ydEW1vBf1DAuoY51XMRTCjKOGXD4i3G9yI7b1bmJ96B4VppxaVUstsMm4bR95P3qxvAWcUHF6CXYtWTHtSgOzQfjHE2cME4EzcIynslvNDBslVaIhl4bLnc2gYNXraG-M4XlJvLexfN1tpRSf-LiJmI2a6O95-FiPnItRExc3pAqTlRTH_PAp7u0Eqm61lpiHhU6oqtG71gQxeB1o2KcaS7e-QnJJpDQIc2Vlrt-6ONvdSj7ImYu2L88cQYsSpNJHvsFuP3q_azlpcRXwEAAP__MdSJOg). The bus was taken to the agency's Bus Operating Facility, where its onboard video [will be reviewed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO5CAQBNCvwRkjaGPjCRxc4mwvvdBqQ3uMFsMK2sPOff1p7hc2Lumpqv58rB8YYkiP33jSzN97XhO1GomZyvqkRH8vili5hE8qhI5DTvVaQcEolZZqWD2G-FpN5-dN-bvuaNZ2tKM2ZjTdMdvJ3gfrpsErAkSarKHe33EcrB16tXVhfmNKq0GD0gputI_ej-DB7qg2g8Io8oFzufFBTN9Y9_jKTyo3l88uzgfzVxX9LwGLgKW1dvs88vUOBSxYOLhIApb3LgFLzA6jgOXIV-Wc5ElcstyuKt8kXyVVeYTH0fAlYZrk6R65UZJn8JxbErDANEjnjbO9JbnryUjjrJGb64101m_aUD8hQVdm_9VCjKGOVhj1ODHE_535Z08_Z_gXAAD__2Nfkpg). A METRO spokesperson praised the "coordination and professionalism" of emergency personnel who "helped ensure a swift response during a challenging situation." A witness who was waiting at a nearby bus stop said she was thankful she and her daughter weren't hurt. "_I'm just shook_ ," [said Latafia Harris](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGy2yAQRb8GdXhgjZFfQZFG3UubUrOCxWYeBg-srOd8fYZM_iDNVjv3nnN_fa6fmHIqt5_4IMffsa6Fjp6Jmdr6okK_d8rYuaUvaoSeUy19X0GBlUpLdVkDpvxezRTcpsKHnsjp2c5WG2PNdHeIl82f0Si9WfzwPvrzHGAzVx3IW22n5EaY0uqiQWkFJ4o2BAsB5ohqMyiMopC4thPfiekbe8zv-qJ28vUxZXdnfnZx_iFgEbAcx3HyOfkvuNe9cy3jS8AytAQsuXrMApbRKWBRehwjYEnlRZ3TDYeh9LVwKjt1iZGpyX9Z8kHcqtz2LiPm3GWNUcL1KgfOE_tomJoLzyPlnLqdhVG3B6b8F5X_b-CXgz8BAAD__6zNkg4). "_I'm traumatized at the same time, but I'm thankful because they could have been us_."
## Lady Raiders Sign 7-Foot-1 Nigerian Player
Stephanie Okechukwu, a 7-foot-1 basketball player from Nigeria, has signed with the No. 21 Texas Tech Lady Raiders. Pending confirmation of her eligibility by the NCAA, she'll become the [tallest woman](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O2zAQhJ-G6miQa-qvYJGG3aVNKazEtc3cmjTItXXK0wc-IE-QZqYZzHyYXx_LByZO-foT7-Tl61KWTHtjEqG6vCjTnycxNqnpkyrhJqnk9lzAwKCN1aZfIiY-FtdFv5o42468HYdxsM4Nrrv52c3gpmjA9RMO5_N6toT9ivPqZhp76JJ_lxlregvGGjjRZYhxgAjjBc3qUDlDMUmpJ7mR0Be2Cx_lRfW0lXvH_ibyaOr8Q0FQEPZ9Pz0bSol4vAMKQpNSj7c_SpWmIOQNcVcQ3sMKgrH_RJCZmugH40FVp6z3cqfc9FaY6Up6xfZJsiKzlqJ_l5T1N5EW2m4KwjTOk3PzZMyoIHTVx8eemFMbRuXM9Y6Jv6nl_75-efgbAAD__5Q9kp8) to play college basketball. Okechukwu, who has no prior collegiate or professional experience, has been recruited by several major programs, including Big 12 rival West Virginia. She speaks Igbo, Japanese, and English, and she plans to [major in business](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL2yoyAYfRrsyAAiakGxjV223dJB-IiMCBn4jMk-_R3zCrc7M-fM-ft3n-8mxJAef80OGt8-zwnOGgERyvyCBP8PiKZiCRsUMBZDTvWYBROKMk5ZNzsT4meWjdMLcyNvQPNe9YpLqWSz6hb8CJINvVLA277t7MD9OHrbdqIbBmiCvswYZx0XjDNxA6-cU8KJ3hu2SEMkAxcwlxuugPA21cdPfkG52bw3Ua-Iz0raP0RMRExfAYJdL5aI6ZpDxHRlEDHxCxIxnXmHVOli6ga4mBhpNO5DiwkOSqU1PBLtqc8ZKacWEkKhFeG5mhSA5g3semzn0RTtnmeIMVTVE8keuwnxWwt_d-ZLi58AAAD__0BTikI). On New Year's Eve, Texas Tech coach Krista Gerlich posted a [video](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDtu6zAQAE9DdTZ2VyRlFSweXqBUTptSoMSVRYQWDXL9y-kD5wppp5gB5vM4Hn1McTt9-DM7eSx53PheE4twGW-88feVk69S4hcX9rPEvNXrSEB2B7gDMwYf03PUTXAThB4bdtjZzqLWVjerY80TsdGTJwSzYH9gMj1yPxnULZomupcMEAwSINCeFxuCpUDd4mHSXmngECWXvaws_PB1Sc9847Kf87lJbhW5VNX-UzQoGh4vqmj4n_28vnNJcV4VDVW8XKuigQCs6cmi7izZw8EQqnaoqn0jaIoLl3tMKVbbKQ2ns4_ptyJ_e3Nz9BMAAP__Qft1Fw) of Okechukwu walking through an airport with the caption, "_Walking into the New Year feeling 7 feet tall!_ " A [video](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuqzAUgOGnMVvQ8cGxyeDhSldUHdKlUjsig4-DVQORfRJCn75KX6H7r2_4P8_92cUUl8ubm8nyI6z9QltJxEy5v9NC3zdKrnCOX5TJjRzXpdx6BNQHkAc49t7FtPeq8nYAf5IVWWm00VIprarJaq2HVramwXDyNJixoROMxmPr2xFCU0X7xEDCUSJIwJqC9l6jRxMcDMoJBeQjr7nmiZgeroS0r3fK9bjOVbIT87WI5p_ATmC3bVsdl8Lukt38LAR2mSgJ7P6_7K_T7X3_MA-BXZWtv24xpVi0EQous4vpl-S_jbhb_AkAAP__tJxy5g) posted to Okechukwu’s Instagram shows her dunking with ease. Fourteen people were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, but all of them are now "safe and out of danger," according to an agency spokesperson. The bus was stuck on an embankment next to McGowen Street and State Highway 288 for several hours after the crash. While the driver and most passengers were able to exit the bus on their own, firefighters had to use the Jaws of Life and "almost every cutting tool that we had" to . The bus was taken to the agency's Bus Operating Facility, where its onboard video . A METRO spokesperson praised the "coordination and professionalism" of emergency personnel who "helped ensure a swift response during a challenging situation." A witness who was waiting at a nearby bus stop said she was thankful she and her daughter weren't hurt. "I'm just shook ," . "I'm traumatized at the same time, but I'm thankful because they could have been us." name: The Maine Wire
subject: Trump Forces the Overpass Romantics into the Arms of Nicolás Maduro
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Trump Forces the Overpass Romantics into the Arms of Nicolás Maduro (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=1322f1d5fb&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Tom Shattuck on Jan 04, 2026 07:20 pm
Aging “No Kings” Boomers and nose-ring Columbia Intifada enthusiasts alike have a new boyfriend and his name is Nicolás Maduro. His thick, Stalin coif and mustache press all the right buttons for American progressives who adore anyone seen as oppositional to President Donald J. Trump. Many of the Madurosexuals hated the deposed Venezuelan dictator only [...]

The post Trump Forces the Overpass Romantics into the Arms of Nicolás Maduro (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=55e7a9ac35&e=f093fa355c) appeared first on The Maine Wire (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=668185bac0&e=f093fa355c) .
Read the full story » (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=e0bab63244&e=f093fa355c)

** Mills Emerges on Senate Trail as Gateway Scandal Looms, Silence Continues (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=c454f8f624&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Jan 03, 2026 05:02 pm
Gov. Janet Mills stepped back into the public eye as two Democrats seeking to challenge Republican U.S. Sen. Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | First Invеstmеnt Updаtеs brоught tо уоu bу Іnception Media, LLC. Elizabeth at the Chandler City Council meeting.

**Chandler City Council Testimony** Last month, Elizabeth testified in front of the Chandler City Council to voice opposition to the proposed construction of a new, 40,000 square foot data center at the corner of Price Road that would not guarantee job creation and could seriously harm the environment. The Council unanimously rejected the proposal! This is the result of a people-powered movement of neighbors coming together to protect the community. **You can watch Elizabeth 's testimony ****[here](https://clicksp.nurselee4az.com/f/a/-8Di0MVI4UvEU7oTsY9MJg~~/AAPPABA~/KWx8cUVqBx6JHGQGcVS8PpwMrx4w4DRjF9BeMk4XFBlk4IyVPUhzP4PWZXi65ayzfbwQhULPReITVf155H6XBQyalG21TjdiA-qlAe78-peNXNQuxscKKHV-c0RO4rbwtqXIF2q3JP3-HpAcEzQoUn55DqNUp8nddVkQYFsewJdee5mMcANdE4xJYpaYdMtjd7xFWgV5CsZwhrsmtKhSvGCedGte55X6V5up035qhsp-4AAozizeucdVW-JLVJRylrsDWewULVSXrwUihOPDjg~~). ** **Mesa City Council Testimony** Likewise, Elizabeth spoke up for the folks she met on the campaign trail in Mesa at the Mesa City Council Meeting early in December. The Council was conducting a hearing on the 287(g) agreement between the Mesa Police Department and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). clean_body: | | Putting People Before Politics, Issue 5 ~ ~ Dear Peter, Happy New Year! 🚀 2026 is all about taking part in the change YOU want to see happen in AZ05. Here's your chance to make a huge difference. You 're invited! Become a member of the Founding Circle of Friends! 💙 Become a member of the Founding Circle of Friends by being one of the first 250 people to donate $100, or more, to this important campaign. Initial contributions are crucial to launching this campaign and winning a seat at the table where Elizabeth will "put people before politics!" In Elizabeth Lee , and in this pivotable time, we finally have a chance to change the direction for our community and to have a representative who will fight for us. Founding members will receive exclusive updates from behind the scenes of this critical campaign. In ourselves and in Elizabeth, we have a chance to be the change we want to see. Please sign up (~) right away to make sure to be one of the 250 members of the Founding Circle of Friends. | Call for Volunteers: We need YOU! The campaign is kicking it into high gear in 2026. In Phase 2 of the campaign, we are expanding our team to ensure Elizabeth gets on the ballot. The campaign can benefit from YOUR talents! It doesn't matter if you're an introvert, extrovert, or new to a political campaign. The team can benefit from your most precious resource: TIME. Join the team and be part of the "Putting People Before Politics" movement. The campaign will be participating in the upcoming Jan. 19th Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. (MLK ) Day parade in Mesa.~ The Mesa MLK Day parade is an awesome opportunity to show up for the campaign, whether you want to actually walk in the parade with Elizabeth, or help at the event table. Here's a quick rundown of what's required for the MLK event to be a success: 2-3 volunteers per shift (no walking in parade)-SWAG AND SIGNATURES TENT Shift A: 9:00am -12:00pm set up and tabling Shift B:12:00pm - 2:00pm tabling Shift C: 2:00pm - 4:00pm tabling and breaking down tables and tent 5-20 volunteers-WALK PARADE WITH ELIZABETH Volunteers will walk with Elizabeth, and our campaign banner, while waving at the crowd (red, navy blue or orange color schemes if you don't have a campaign T-shirt); parade itself is from 11:00am - 12:00pm. Note: 3-4 people are required to carry the banner. ~ If you can't make the parade, but still want to support the event, you can donate a case of bottled water to the campaign. Please email Dimitra, Field Director, dimitralix@gmail.com by Jan 16, 2026. | | Elizabeth 'Nurse' Lee in the News | Elizabeth at the Chandler City Council meeting. Chandler City Council Testimony Last month, Elizabeth testified in front of the Chandler City Council to voice opposition to the proposed construction of a new, 40,000 square foot data center at the corner of Price Road that would not guarantee job creation and could seriously harm the environment. The Council unanimously rejected the proposal! This is the result of a people-powered movement of neighbors coming together to protect the community. You can watch Elizabeth 's testimony ~~. Mesa City Council Testimony Likewise, Elizabeth spoke up for the folks she met on the campaign trail in Mesa at the Mesa City Council Meeting early in December. The Council was conducting a hearing on the 287(g) agreement between the Mesa Police Department and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Elizabeth helped shed light on community concerns that this agreement breeds an environment of fear and takes resources away from the community for unduly harsh immigration measures. You can watch her public comments~~. # PLEASE COMPLETE THIS SURVEY TODAY IF YOU CAN!
---
| | **💥 A Data-Driven Campaign Making History in Ohio 💥**

**STRBICH FOR OHIO
Marcell Strbich for Ohio Secretary of State**

Let’s talk a little inside baseball. ⚾

Many of you have asked about the Voter Identification (Voter ID) and Voter Preference surveys that I (and many others) have been circulating ahead of the May 5, 2026 Republican Primary for Ohio Secretary of State.

What is the point, and why does your participation matter?

Historically, political outsiders face steep odds when challenging the hand-picked, musical-chairs candidates in Columbus. The obstacles are predictable: inadequate fundraising and amateur campaign execution.

That is not the case here.

Marcell Strbich has done nearly everything right.

>From the time he entered the race through the June 30, 2025 filing deadline, Marcell outraised Robert Sprague - his only declared primary opponent. Based on current intelligence, there is a strong likelihood he will meet or exceed Sprague’s second-half 2025 fundraising as well. That puts Marcell in a rare position for a challenger: a serious war chest heading into February and March, just ahead of April early voting.

Equally important, Marcell is backed by experienced, professional political consultants who have designed a highly targeted, data-driven digital and direct-mail campaign.

Their modeling shows Marcell needs approximately 560,000 votes to win the primary. To achieve that, the campaign must initially target about 1.2 million likely Republican primary voters.

This is where the Voter ID and Voter Preference surveys become essential - and urgent.

Every supporter we identify before mid-February is one fewer voter the campaign must pay to persuade through advertising and mail. If we identify:

• 300,000 Strbich supporters, the paid universe drops to 900,000 voters - making the ad budget 25% more effective.

• 400,000 supporters, effectiveness increases by 33%.

• 560,000 supporters, the primary is effectively over, and resources can be preserved for the general election.

That is how campaigns win efficiently.

To be absolutely clear: THIS SURVEY IS NOT A DATA-HARVESTING OR SPAM OPERATION.

YOU WILL NOT BE BLASTED WITH CALLS, EMAILS, OR TEXTS. ADDITIONAL CONTACT ONLY OCCURS IF YOU EXPLICITLY REQUEST INFORMATION AND VOLUNTARILY PROVIDE CONTACT DETAILS.

The only required information is:

• Your survey responses
• Your first and last name
• Your county

That’s it.

Volunteers use this information solely to locate you on the voter file and classify you as:

• Supporting Marcell Strbich
• Supporting another candidate
• Undecided

This is how every dollar goes further - and why grassroots participation matters.

Please take a moment to complete the [**SURVEY YOU CAN FIND HERE**](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=da337e6d3f&e=5c740fa3e2). Then share it widely. Duplicate submissions are not a problem - we have safeguards in place. Text it. Email it. Post it. SEND IT EVERYWHERE.

This is how we win!

THANK YOU!

clean_body: Contribute to our survey on safeguarding elections. | | | | | | PLEASE READ THIS AND CONSIDER ACTING! IT WILL ONLY TAKE MINUTES BUT IS EXTREMELY IMPORTANT! Thank you for subscribing & staying subscribed! BE SURE TO HAVE YOUR FAMILY & FRIENDS "JOIN US": | "Join Us!" | | | PLEASE COMPLETE THIS SURVEY TODAY IF YOU CAN! | | 💥 A Data-Driven Campaign Making History in Ohio 💥 STRBICH FOR OHIO Marcell Strbich for Ohio Secretary of State Let’s talk a little inside baseball. ⚾ Many of you have asked about the Voter Identification (Voter ID) and Voter Preference surveys that I (and many others) have been circulating ahead of the May 5, 2026 Republican Primary for Ohio Secretary of State. What is the point, and why does your participation matter? Historically, political outsiders face steep odds when challenging the hand-picked, musical-chairs candidates in Columbus. The obstacles are predictable: inadequate fundraising and amateur campaign execution. That is not the case here. Marcell Strbich has done nearly everything right. From the time he entered the race through the June 30, 2025 filing deadline, Marcell outraised Robert Sprague - his only declared primary opponent. Based on current intelligence, there is a strong likelihood he will meet or exceed Sprague’s second-half 2025 fundraising as well. That puts Marcell in a rare position for a challenger: a serious war chest heading into February and March, just ahead of April early voting. Equally important, Marcell is backed by experienced, professional political consultants who have designed a highly targeted, data-driven digital and direct-mail campaign. Their modeling shows Marcell needs approximately 560,000 votes to win the primary. To achieve that, the campaign must initially target about 1.2 million likely Republican primary voters. This is where the Voter ID and Voter Preference surveys become essential - and urgent. Every supporter we identify before mid-February is one fewer voter the campaign must pay to persuade through advertising and mail. If we identify: • 300,000 Strbich supporters, the paid universe drops to 900,000 voters - making the ad budget 25% more effective. • 400,000 supporters, effectiveness increases by 33%. • 560,000 supporters, the primary is effectively over, and resources can be preserved for the general election. That is how campaigns win efficiently. To be absolutely clear: THIS SURVEY IS NOT A DATA-HARVESTING OR SPAM OPERATION. YOU WILL NOT BE BLASTED WITH CALLS, EMAILS, OR TEXTS. ADDITIONAL CONTACT ONLY OCCURS IF YOU EXPLICITLY REQUEST INFORMATION AND VOLUNTARILY PROVIDE CONTACT DETAILS. The only required information is: • Your survey responses • Your first and last name • Your county That’s it. Volunteers use this information solely to locate you on the voter file and classify you as: • Supporting Marcell Strbich • Supporting another candidate • Undecided This is how every dollar goes further - and why grassroots participation matters. Please take a moment to complete the . Then share it widely. Duplicate submissions are not a problem - we have safeguards in place. Text it. Email it. Post it. SEND IT EVERYWHERE. This is how we win! THANK YOU! | | | | | GFI NEWSLETTER ARCHIVE | | Thank you for being a GFI person as part of the GFI network. We're always interested in how we can do a better job of connecting patriots across Ohio. If you want to communicate, we're here! Best regards, Mark Pukita A GFI Person mark@gfiohio.com 614-499-0599 (c) | | Copyright © 2026 The Grassroots Freedom Initiative, All rights reserved.

| | [](https://substack..IRuTei5Q3cN0UOps7UkWmtyXCAb74rRVOhg10ePQK2c?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/maduro?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Well, Trump decided to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and his wife in the dark of night over the weekend.
One reason to celebrate is that Maduro's extraction happened so dramatically with apparently no American casualties. That's a phenomenal achievement by the Special Forces.
I confess to being deeply conflicted and ambivalent about this action. On one hand, Maduro is a bad guy, an autocrat and a driver of the international drug trade. Venezuela has been such a troubled country that millions have already fled as refugees, including to the U.S. Millions celebrated his ouster, which was facilitated by military leaders there. The oil industry in Venezuela is functioning at maybe 25% of its potential due to poor management, sanctions and Maduro being something of a pariah. Unlocking that potential could be a massive boon to the standard of living of millions of Venezuelans and the entire region.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f164c55d-526a-4a6f-8f08-a6ea4c1e21aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
On the other hand, the Trump crew doesn't seem to have a great plan for the follow-through. It's a unilateral action taken without Congressional approval or notification. It kicks off a "law of the jungle" era where foreign leaders can be kidnapped by superpowers. It sets a real 'might makes right' precedent in one's sphere of influence that bodes poorly for what's to come.
Trump came to power in part as a backlash against some of the nation-building misadventures in Afghanistan and Iraq. Now we may be doing it again in Venezuela.
As you know, I ran for President. I sometimes reflect on what I would do in various situations. I'm not someone who would rule out intervening if there's a malignant actor who is harming Americans and his own people through corruption and criminality. Apparently, Maduro was given multiple warnings and off-ramps. Sometimes, action is preferable to inaction. I yet regard America as a force for good in the world.
In most things though, what matters most is not whether it's a good idea or not but how you execute, stick around and follow through. I joke that I sometimes have a good idea and then spend 5 years trying to make it happen. There's always a chance you make a bad situation worse. Running a country from a boardroom thousands of miles away is unrealistic.
Will America be willing to engage in a way that improves life on the ground for the people of Venezuela and the region for the next 5 - 10 years? That's the key, unanswered question. Time will tell. I have the feeling that Trump will be long gone by the time we know the answer, while millions will be living with the consequences.
If you want to save money in '26, go to [noblemobile.com/yang](https://substack.com/redirect/c2e32edd-6a00-404f-a2b6-7aa9895ff184?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for 3 months off your wireless bill, the best deal around. My new [book](https://substack.com/redirect/980aa34f-ab37-4efc-b0d8-62051b34156d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) "Hey Yang, Where's my Thousand Bucks?" comes out in February and the tour dates are up [here](https://substack.com/redirect/3b677f0f-7695-45af-87f6-7ee27b06fb3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Use code 'UBIUBI' for 25% [off](https://substack.com/redirect/980aa34f-ab37-4efc-b0d8-62051b34156d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the book. I'm also celebrating my bday on Jan. 15that our [Offline Party](https://substack.com/redirect/cfa730b7-8f9a-42f3-8ded-023ec805eadb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in NYC so come find me there! Let's make '26 a year to remember!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f638358d-6263-4f39-b804-32910d311d1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

name: Dave McCormick
subject: How an early morning church service strengthened my faith
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick
body:
This is a deeply personal story

| | | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z51f87pfor0lr5oo6mm6q69fq5cpt2276g8pf7j8_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)
---
| | I want to take a moment today to share a personal story with you, Friend
It’s a story about how an early morning church service and a sermon referencing Psalm 121 left an imprint on my soul and strengthened my faith.
When I arrived at West Point as a new cadet, I had no idea how much it would transform my life.
During my first plebe summer, I attended an early morning church service. The sermon referenced Psalm 121:
**“I lift my eyes to the mountains- where does my help come from? My help comes from the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.” Psalm 121**
**That psalm and my time at West Point have been a touchstone ever since, sustaining me during difficult moments in my life.**
When I decided to run for U.S. Senate, I made a promise to come to Washington and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up business as usual. Now I’m working to fulfill that promise every single day.
We need leaders in DC with the strength, the will, and the moral clarity to meet the challenges we face. **So, if you’re looking to continue the status quo, I’m not your guy.**
But if you want to help me continue my fight to change the way things are done in DC, I need your support. Our average online donation is $21.38, and **_[I would be so grateful if you would generously give that amount today.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zvdojrhm9nneboijvh1745o1ln1ghf51e5o5bntg_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)_**
I’m no stranger to fighting for what is right, and ****if we don’t continue pushing every single day to clean up the mess the career politicians made, we will lose America as we know it!****
There are too many pressing issues we must solve to hold back now. Whether it’s illegal immigration and the fentanyl crisis or inflation and the cost of living, we cannot afford to lose ground.
Here’s where I need your help, Friend I hate asking for money — I’m not a career politician, and it’s not in my nature — but I have no choice but to ask if we’re going to continue fighting to advance our cause to change how things are done.
Democrats from all over the country are panicking and pulling out all the stops to attack Republicans who are shaking up Washington. That’s why I’m hoping you can spare **_[$25, $50, or even $100 to help me fight back.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2znk42niqa5bnpodls0gp5mks0b7l48hobcdj1cag_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)_**
---
[DONATE](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2znlhcj4mo0g8jo67n85t586v15kq5arpop0t110o_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)
| | Please, join me today to fix our broken government and save what makes America worth fighting for.
Thank you,Senator Dave McCormickArmy Veteran, USMA'87
---
[DONATE](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zo8a8o7s5g4o2anpsn5618jeusus140hobrokh4g_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)
| | |
---
| | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z8tuo365jklg65lma25rpnm4stv7m8s3saec25ng_rp22sh2s8i6eor7cdj66o34chh2bs)
---
| | I'm Dave McCormick. I'm a West Point grad, combat veteran who served in the 82nd Airborne, a 7th-generation Pennsylvanian, **and last November I defeated an entrenched career politician in the #1 swing state of the 2024 election.**
I’ve seen firsthand what great leadership looks like. I’ve seen that even on our worst days, you have to put the needs of others first.
That’s why I made a promise to come to the Senate and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up Washington, and I’m working to do that every single day.
Whether it’s securing the border, reducing crime in our cities, ending the fentanyl epidemic, or bringing down the cost of living–**I’m committed to delivering concrete solutions to the biggest issues we face.**
But in order to get the job done, it’s going to take all hands on deck. I need every freedom-loving American to help me shake up Washington. I promise I won't let you down.
_Senator Dave McCormick_
---

name: Stephen, Legacy House GOP Memberships
subject: You are one of the first to hear about this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We’ve just opened this for our top supporters!

Friend - I've been WAITING to tell you the news!

# 2026 Candidates: Letters of Intent
---
|
---
| |

Friend,
2026 is a crucial election cycle for the Republican Party. As we put 2025 behind us, our focus must shift forward to the 2026 midterm elections and the opportunities ahead.
In Gloucester County, control of the Board of Commissioners is back up for election, with three seats on the ballot. This election will be critical to the future direction of our county and our state.
The Gloucester County Republican Party is now accepting Letters of Intent for the following offices:
• United States Senate• United States House of Representatives, District 1 (CD-1)• Gloucester County Commissioner
All Letters of Intent must be submitted to gcgopnj@gmail.com, no later than January 30th at 4:00 PM.
If you have any questions or need additional information, please reach out to us.
Together, let’s prepare, organize, and win in 2026. 🇺🇸
Best,
_Chairman Wingate_ Gloucester County GOP

name: Ali For Congress
subject: Accountability Matters
body:
It's time to hold Alma Adams accountable for her failed leadership.
| | **Can 't see this message? _[View in browser](https://aliforcongress.com/so/c8PkEI63L/c?w=ikVLQaywNYaRS9px3BXbilBiiU6pwmU83sktsn_lsHI.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hbGlmb3Jjb25ncmVzcy5jb20vc28vYzhQa0VJNjNMP2xhbmd1YWdlVGFnPWVuIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiMjg2YWY5N2QtNzMxZC00NzYyLThkYjktZWQwMDYyYjQ3M2RjIn0)_**
---
| | |
---
| |
# ACCOUNTABILITY MATTERS
---
| |
---
| | Around the world, we’re seeing what happens when leaders are finally held accountable for corruption, oppression, and abuse of power. The message is clear: no one should be above the law.Yet here at home, Washington politicians like Alma Adams stay silent when it’s convenient, look the other way when it’s political, and refuse to stand up to failed policies that weaken America and empower our enemies.I’m not running for Congress to stay quiet.I’m calling out the failures—whether it’s foreign dictators propped up by weak U.S. leadership or career politicians who won’t answer to the people they represent. Leadership means having the courage to speak plainly and act decisively.That’s exactly what I’ve done before. In one of the deepest blue districts in North Carolina, I took on the establishment and earned over 90,000 votes. Now, I’m doing it again—because this district deserves a Representative who will fight, not fade into the background.But I can’t do it alone.Your support today helps us take this message to every voter in the 12th District, hold Alma Adams accountable, and send a clear signal that the 12th District is ready for principled, conservative leadership.If you believe accountability matters—at home and abroad—I’m asking you to stand with me today. Please contribute today [here](https://aliforcongress.com/so/c8PkEI63L/c?w=RD--L2bU5Hpu6M677_nT_lScNcD-oK1cjWIvkGOZpJc.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9hbGktZm9yLWNvbmdyZXNzL2RvbmF0ZSIsInIiOiI5YWM4YmZlZS1hN2M5LTRjMzAtOGZmMC1iYWQ3ZDRlNWQyNDkiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIyODZhZjk3ZC03MzFkLTQ3NjItOGRiOS1lZDAwNjJiNDczZGMifQ)Let’s finish what we started.Gratefully,
Addul Ali
Candidate for U.S. House, NC-12

name: Councilor Sue AnderBois via ActionNetwork.org
subject: Kicking off 2026
body:
| | | [](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMQRjame2DNSYCRFyBn4a_01pee00t_I5pRD-RHrm5MtCGxKMFnjxCXIzOGC0bhvXYK50SnOLnpfuuJn64PIFYI15CHdzwaTqvoNBJYSyKxPNQ7cZL0tUtU4cLkgy50SU0lXDb9_cJh9NcMZimv0Cd3n6mLSMmVD8tHr2m2zeFywmjC0oLGeIA4qqOzMmdVqjUUNpcg2tfZBazoGm1irX2quCYHGBgvQ5KKJeBCVTHdZE/4n1/MHFRw6kOQ5O6wvTGfYajtA/h0/h001.J03ub89sTAn4zaBUZDAX-cNxmyIaw8HK9WM9eUJzKN4) Hello Friends & Neighbors, Happy 2026! I hope that this first newsletter of the year finds you well-rested, with hope and some optimism for the year to come. I tried to catch up on some reading during the quieter week between Christmas and New Years -- and read "Life After Cars" and "On Tyranny" -- and am mid-way through Jane Jacobs' "Dark Age Ahead". What are you reading -- and what should be on my list for 2026? As always, there's a lot going on - so I'll just jump right in! This newsletter (and all archived newsletters) can be found at: https://www.sueanderbois.com/post/kicking-off-2026 _**Upcoming Meetings & Community Events**:_
* _Christmas Tree Pick up!_ Is your tree getting dry? Christmas tree pick up is provided by the City starting this week's trash cycle. Remove all ornaments, lights, tinsel, etc from your tree -- and place it on your curb next to your trash for pick up.
* _January Council Meetings_ : The full City Council will me at 6pm on Thursday the 8th and Thursday the 22nd this month. (Usually it's the 1rst and 3rd Thursday of the month, but was updated because of New Years Day).
* _Indivisible January 6th Vigil, 5:30 at the Statehouse_ : Our friends at Indivisible have organized a pro-democracy vigil to remember and reflect on the insurrection that happened on January 6, 2021. I have been invited to share a few remarks on the impact of January 6th and reflect on where are & where we are headed.

* Save RIPTA Rally and Press Conference, Thursday January 8th at 3pm - State House Library: I know many Ward 3 residents are concerned about the service cuts to RIPTA. Check out the Save RIPTA rally and press conference this Thursday to learn more and get involved this legislative session. If you haven't been to the State House before - the library is on the second floor -- you can walk right up the center staircase to access it.

* _The Alcove Grand Opening - Saturday, January 10th 12-4_ : Congratulations to The Alcove (formerly Gather RI) on their Grand Opening this coming weekend - and to our ward 3 neighbor Anne on spearheading this effort! The Alcove was created to foster "community for women and gender expansive people through public programs and a member Haven - a space blending portraits, stories, and gathering areas designed for renewal, connection, and shared momentum." See more about the opening [here](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.SNYHID0cgDJeR88MOfSMucLRQLl29aZwmbI9R8h9PvENAXQuH4wjBgYJQs4iPIG_kFXXyKEjJq7eKJrOXlIgc-MoHOoxRgzGzUrZJWlvzOXHY4ij3n_ElAU_CIA6aSLyw_mT67zB60dKU6lHrKzaUma0qNOFV0S5JolchtyelW7pHzdA1w8Rvlg-3szhMOMqjQgl8Jxk43Jl5wjzUfZaXIBxsippS7y23WHJNo8jfxw1ltRre5Ta23yNG_iJSL-w1Un290h0hnBlD-B7NIHk9U75XnOZkYeOsrLHrcFuL79UcTBCEOviteT-TgRBnkCi1pBSBGNOau7EnDf0BiP-zrjrkr0D0BFtKethunbO1FMwoMjrFgFACks1ID7OmUX9GDf5Tt1MFTSCoChL2p9QqinDUhJjQtDJu2qh_33hqoSowfL_robsc_4U6sJ7RHps3_yWIeEgI3SPQoWh2AsLZMSqhgnr8wQ-oKOTHdZF4rCuLSSwanSGgtqkgRWZvCtxji5xFrENp0-6z4DJZ4CY8ORmRfmVaIveboSMcpBTjSmaU0r6nwwoDJGRSNf2DGQmMu3SapD36q5ZE9AAiqSv3s_7LzoKNQGU_1-bbCuxN13PaPubjq0ndBe3rPePdibKfIxGp8FpCC-2owWZE8YcV8p6fjxfcGQf5ey0SeDg9HWr0B50AjfE3aIXdGilTn9ZH0O68z3IVWyg1QSG63W5tYouIzHuNK70EF1XyKSAm2a-Sp9BRRJJbbYwLLv5ue6l8SsciCzuLtYwW8NEM-oIKg/4n1/MHFRw6kOQ5O6wvTGfYajtA/h1/h001.ZbPhlpEbcj8O0-6FVQLHTAJOMeIwKO9jT3dQMyTKSmA).

**_Additional Updates:_**
* _Stormwater Management_ : The City's Director of Sustainability Priscilla De La Cruz led a year-long Stormwater Task Force in 2024 (I was on this Task Force.). The Task Force released a report and memo in 2025. I asked Director De La Cruz to present the Task Force's report to my Special Committee on the Environment and Resilience late last year -- the report and the presentation are both available on the City's Open Meetings Portal [here](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.lmpSTrE-G07FKUPTdpC9IYucVZyv3P9zoeIJOjNiisnlidBD3Owmzws2LO8-4OI54RSXfADGfvSvFXnFDIA0PiZJjgAn1b8xsivmCEramOtjqVaOwz6LHjpSfpdPupMQn-CyJu6-lZ7QcMZs0hGZN3YIrOdmGKTwcbtJoe_4riTMaatqQ6fBeBRgi_T8Wr18RB-msIE2dPx8r4y10QrNhEMAjnVHfi2X93PpSmwmkfyxypJCG55eH5sfTSOAIpNBhA7TB_VyK_vHIIVR0J4RASnmUBlBKm836nHgRnOqWAU/4n1/MHFRw6kOQ5O6wvTGfYajtA/h2/h001.Rxi6FlTyzPPAuy2UZ0WHN068yKQfcCIBbHNxsBJ20Zw). I will be working with my colleagues this year on an ordinance to address some of the concerns raised in the report.
* _Rent Stabilization_ : There was inaccurate reporting that a proposal for rent stabilization will be on our January 8th Council agenda. However, keeping rents down is a topic that is important to me and many of my colleagues -- and I know many of you! -- and there will likely be a proposal introduced sometime this winter. I will share a link to it and the ways to testify and engage when that has been introduced. Thank you to the many folks who have reached out to me already with their thoughts and opinions!
* _North Main Street - New Lights_ : Our lighting projects on North Main are finally fully energized! In the last year, I led a $500,000 project to address lighting concerns identified by the North Main Street Task Force - and we had now added pedestrian-level lighting near University Heights Marketplace, overhead lighting in the corridor near North Burial Ground, and additional lighting in the business corridor north of 3rd St. Like all things on North Main, this required coordination with many entities -- RIDOT, the City's Forester, RI Energy, the Public Property Department, our contractor Prism, and of course the Council's amazing project director Kyle Delgado. When you bring folks together, you can get done things! Nothing ever moves as quickly as I would like -- but I was thrilled to see the final lights turned on this weekend -- and walked parts of the corridor to check it out! clean_body: | | | Hello Friends & Neighbors, Happy 2026! I hope that this first newsletter of the year finds you well-rested, with hope and some optimism for the year to come. I tried to catch up on some reading during the quieter week between Christmas and New Years -- and read "Life After Cars" and "On Tyranny" -- and am mid-way through Jane Jacobs' "Dark Age Ahead". What are you reading -- and what should be on my list for 2026? As always, there's a lot going on - so I'll just jump right in! This newsletter (and all archived newsletters) can be found at: Upcoming Meetings & Community Events: Christmas Tree Pick up! Is your tree getting dry? Christmas tree pick up is provided by the City starting this week's trash cycle. Remove all ornaments, lights, tinsel, etc from your tree -- and place it on your curb next to your trash for pick up. January Council Meetings : The full City Council will me at 6pm on Thursday the 8th and Thursday the 22nd this month. (Usually it's the 1rst and 3rd Thursday of the month, but was updated because of New Years Day). Indivisible January 6th Vigil, 5:30 at the Statehouse : Our friends at Indivisible have organized a pro-democracy vigil to remember and reflect on the insurrection that happened on January 6, 2021. I have been invited to share a few remarks on the impact of January 6th and reflect on where are & where we are headed. Save RIPTA Rally and Press Conference, Thursday January 8th at 3pm - State House Library: I know many Ward 3 residents are concerned about the service cuts to RIPTA. Check out the Save RIPTA rally and press conference this Thursday to learn more and get involved this legislative session. If you haven't been to the State House before - the library is on the second floor -- you can walk right up the center staircase to access it. The Alcove Grand Opening - Saturday, January 10th 12-4 : Congratulations to The Alcove (formerly Gather RI) on their Grand Opening this coming weekend - and to our ward 3 neighbor Anne on spearheading this effort! The Alcove was created to foster "community for women and gender expansive people through public programs and a member Haven - a space blending portraits, stories, and gathering areas designed for renewal, connection, and shared momentum." See more about the opening . Additional Updates: Stormwater Management : The City's Director of Sustainability Priscilla De La Cruz led a year-long Stormwater Task Force in 2024 (I was on this Task Force.). The Task Force released a report and memo in 2025. I asked Director De La Cruz to present the Task Force's report to my Special Committee on the Environment and Resilience late last year -- the report and the presentation are both available on the City's Open Meetings Portal . I will be working with my colleagues this year on an ordinance to address some of the concerns raised in the report. Rent Stabilization : There was inaccurate reporting that a proposal for rent stabilization will be on our January 8th Council agenda. However, keeping rents down is a topic that is important to me and many of my colleagues -- and I know many of you! -- and there will likely be a proposal introduced sometime this winter. I will share a link to it and the ways to testify and engage when that has been introduced. Thank you to the many folks who have reached out to me already with their thoughts and opinions! North Main Street - New Lights : Our lighting projects on North Main are finally fully energized! In the last year, I led a $500,000 project to address lighting concerns identified by the North Main Street Task Force - and we had now added pedestrian-level lighting near University Heights Marketplace, overhead lighting in the corridor near North Burial Ground, and additional lighting in the business corridor north of 3rd St. Like all things on North Main, this required coordination with many entities -- RIDOT, the City's Forester, RI Energy, the Public Property Department, our contractor Prism, and of course the Council's amazing project director Kyle Delgado. When you bring folks together, you can get done things! Nothing ever moves as quickly as I would like -- but I was thrilled to see the final lights turned on this weekend -- and walked parts of the corridor to check it out! (No one has ever been so giddy about street lights as I was!) Social Media : I post regular updates about events, notices, and things I'm up to on social media -- which is a great way to keep in the loop between newsletters! You can follow me on , , and . (I was briefly back on Twitter/X, but it truly does not seem like a place to actually engage/share information and was not a productive use of limited time). Campaign for Lt. Governor Updates! We are hiring! As we've built out our team this fall, we are now looking for a passionate and energetic campaign manager to join the team! Is this you? Or someone you know? See for the full description! Kick off Friend & Fundraiser - : Come kick off the year and raise a glass to our amazing state while helping to elect Sue to be our next LG! Can't attend, but would like to donate anyway? Link here: or checks can be sent to Friends of Sue AnderBois at 166 5th St, Providence 02906 _Gratitude** : As we kick off 2026, I am so grateful to serve you as your City Councilor. Government service is an honor I take seriously. And while I am so upset every day by the actions of our federal government right now -- I am grateful for the opportunity to demonstrate that government can and should care and work hard every day for our communities and the people in them! Please reach out any time by replying to this email if I can be helpful in any way. With Gratitude, Councilor Sue AnderBois Sue AnderBoisCandidate, RI Lt. name: Haley Stevens
subject: I need your help, null
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
Can I quickly share why this is so important?

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11372?t=2&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)
---
Null, in a moment, I'm going to ask you to become a 2026 Founding Donor of my grassroots campaign to keep Michigan blue. **But first, let me explain why your support today is so important.**
Growing up, I was just a kid who _loved_ her home state and wanted to help people.

It's why I answered the call to serve as chief of staff of the Obama administration’s auto rescue, which helped save 200,000 Michigan jobs during the Great Recession.

It's why I ran for Congress – to fight for Michigan families in Washington.

And now, it's why I'm running for Senate – because I love my state and the people who live here, so I refuse to sit back as MAGA chaos harms our families and communities. **But null, I simply can't win without the grassroots support of folks like you, so I have to ask:**
**[Will you chip in $5 or more right now to become a 2026 Founding Donor of my campaign to keep Michigan blue and take back the Senate for Democrats?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11373?t=3&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)**
| | [Donate Now](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11373?t=4&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)
---
I need to be upfront with you: Michigan is now ranked one of the most likely Senate seats to flip. That's why MAGA megadonors are already spending big to flip Michigan red in 2026 – and they’re just getting started.
**If I'm going to go up against the deep pockets of the GOP and win, I'm going to need every bit of grassroots support I can get – especially now that we're officially in the election year. Every day from here on out is crucial, which means we can't miss a single goal.**
**[So please, before tonight's midnight deadline, will you chip in $5 to become one of the final 2026 Founding Donors we need to reach our goal, kickstart the election year, and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11373?t=5&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)**
Thank you,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11374?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11372?t=7&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=8&akid=1844%2E49239%2E2enr2M)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: JB Pritzker
subject: The stakes in New Hampshire could not be higher
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
#
Peter, it’s Governor JB Pritzker.
I’m emailing you today about New Hampshire’s Senate race because this is serious: **Senate Republicans’ hand-picked candidate, John Sununu, jumped in to defeat Chris Pappas and flip this open Senate seat red.**
**[Before I go any further, I have to ask: Will you split $15 or more between Chris Pappas and State Democrats Committee to keep New Hampshire blue?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99047?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg)**
_Without Chris Pappas winning in New Hampshire, Democrats won’t have a chance at taking back a majority in the Senate._ That’s why this race is being targeted by GOP super PACs and Republicans across the country.
The NRSC — which pours millions into electing Republicans to the Senate — said they’re “all-in” on John Sununu. And polling shows: This race is a _statistical dead heat_ between Chris Pappas and John Sununu.
This means New Hampshire will see historic levels of outside spending, and we have to be ready to fight back.
Chris is a proven fighter for working families — and he’s seen tough campaigns like this before. He knows it takes grassroots support to win a race like this. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Peter.
**[If you’re with me and ready to send Chris Pappas to the Senate, will you split $15 between Chris’s campaign and State Democrats Committee today to help him defend New Hampshire’s seat?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99047?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=3&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=4&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99048?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99047?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg)
---
I know Chris is the type of leader we need in the Senate — **[so thank you for supporting his campaign today.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99047?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg)**
With gratitude,
JB Pritzker
Governor of Illinois
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99047?refcode=em20260105-9905&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=11&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg)

If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.statedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_state-democrats-committee/?t=12&akid=9905%2E614464%2ElZM0dg).

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: I want to be direct with you: this fight isn't over
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122692301/593227232/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NWQwZDE4NS00N2VhLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ham6JSJbejCgDUyz1l0WuK67YkaBq6LGwXPAbTQYeCM=&emci=88d0d185-47ea-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=95d0d185-47ea-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=13185894) Derek, Tomorrow marks five years since the January 6th insurrection—a dark day when we saw the heart of American democracy attacked. A crowd of traitors stormed the U.S. Capitol, put lives in danger, brutalized Capitol Police officers, and tried to overthrow our government. What happened that day was unconscionable. And the threat to our democracy didn’t end when the mob was cleared from the building. **I want to be direct with you: this fight isn’t over—not even close.** Since Donald Trump returned to office, he and his allies have put tremendous pressure on the rule of law. He’s flouted the Constitution—abusing his pardon power, seeking retribution against his foes through law enforcement, and even threatening me and several of my colleagues with death for stating the law, for crying out loud. No matter what Trump or his congressional allies try to do, nothing will deter me from standing up for our democracy, defending our freedoms, and doing my job for the people of Western PA. Quitting just isn’t in our vocabulary here. Derek, Donald Trump made it his top priority on Day One of his presidency to pardon the criminals who stormed the Capitol on January 6, 2021.
Criminals who stormed our Capitol and beat the crap out of cops should be serving time, not being served pardons on Day One of Trump’s presidency.

**That behavior isn’t patriotic, Derek. It’s weak, and it’s pathetic.**
I first swore an oath to defend our Constitution at 17 years old, when I entered the Naval Academy. I swore that same oath again when I was elected to represent Pennsylvania in Congress.
That oath is sacred—and it doesn’t expire. It’s why I’ve stood up to Donald Trump and anyone who continues to undermine our democracy, threaten our rights, and put their own power ahead of the rule of law.
America is worth fighting for. I will always defend our freedoms and protect our Constitution.
